question: A process with BPMN process ID `order` and a signal start event for `order-cancel` is at version 3. Version 4 of `order` (also with a signal start for `order-cancel`) is then deployed. Which signal subscription is active?

options.text: Only the subscription for version 4; deploying version 4 deleted the version 3 subscription and opened a new one.
Both version 3 and version 4 subscriptions; broadcasts can start instances of any deployed version simultaneously.
Only the version 3 subscription; new versions register their signal subscription only after the previous version is deleted.
Neither; signal start events register subscriptions when an instance reaches them at runtime, not at deployment.

a.text: Signal subscriptions only exist for the latest version of a process definition; deploying a new version of the same BPMN process ID deletes the old subscription and opens one for the new version.

b.text: Only the latest version of a given BPMN process ID holds the subscription; older versions do not retain their signal start subscriptions.

c.text: The order is reversed: the new version replaces the subscription on deployment; deletion of the previous version is not required.

d.text: Signal start subscriptions are established at deployment, not when a process instance reaches the event.

explanation: Signal subscriptions for signal start events are tied to the latest deployed version of a process definition (per BPMN process ID). Deploying a new version deletes the prior subscription and opens a new one for the latest version, so broadcasting only starts instances of the newest deployed version.
